title: Physically constrained LightGBM expert with selective monotonicity
summary: A small monotone expert can reduce fold-specific quirks that grouped CV may reward but future races will not.
rationale: A small monotone expert can reduce fold-specific quirks that grouped CV may reward but future races will not. LightGBM explicitly supports monotone constraints and advanced methods, making it feasible to encode only high-confidence physics-like directions.
implementation_hint: Train a separate LightGBM binary expert with monotone constraints on a small subset only: positive on `TyreLife`, `DegPerTyreLap`, filtered latent degradation, and maybe `RaceProgress` within dry-race expert; negative on estimated laps left or pit-loss-normalized urgency where justified. Leave ambiguous features unconstrained. Use it as a low-variance ensemble member, not a replacement.
expected_effect: May slightly lower peak local CV but improve chronology-robust generalization and leaderboard stability by preventing implausible inversions.
risk: Wrong constraints on non-monotone regions, especially around wet tyres or early mandatory-stop behavior, can hurt materially; use regime-specific experts rather than one global constrained model.
